sections.paragraph: ἀμάραντος, ον [s. preceding entry] unfading, in imagery of an unfading eschatological landscape 1 Pt 1:4 (the floral aspect of the amaranth plant is here less dominant than in 5:4, where the image of a ‘wreath’ naturally suggests the use of ἀμαράντινος, which would have been inappropriate for the more broadly conceived ‘inheritance’).
